intro: A joint is where two or more bones meet. The knee is a complex Hinge Joint.
notes: Patella: The kneecap bone that protects the joint.
Cartilage: Reduces friction and acts as a shock absorber.

hotspots.label: Patella
hotspots.description: The Patella protects the front of the knee joint. It is a sesamoid bone, meaning it is embedded inside a tendon.
hotspots.examTip: It acts as a pulley to increase the force of the thigh muscles.

hotspots.label: Tendon
hotspots.description:
Tendons are tough, white cords that attach Muscles to Bones (T-M-B).

When a muscle contracts, it pulls on the tendon, which then pulls the bone to move it.

hotspots.label: Cartilage
hotspots.description: Smooth, slippery tissue covering the ends of bones. It reduces friction and absorbs shock during walking or jumping.
hotspots.examTip: Without cartilage, bones would grind against each other causing pain.

hotspots.label: Ligament
hotspots.description:
Ligaments are strong, elastic bands that connect Bone to Bone (L-B-B).

They hold the joint together and prevent it from dislocating.
